summary: Dual gate+up INT4 GEMV is correctly fused. The gemv_int4_dual_fused kernel eliminates 2 separate device.memset calls by using persistent FP32 accumulator buffers similar to the single GEMV fused kernel. Engine uses the fused dual kernel exclusively.

notes: gemv_int4_dual.hip contains gemv_int4_dual_fused kernel using same persistent-buffer pattern as gemv_int4_v2_fused.
Engine _launch_ffn_gate_up_silu uses the fused dual kernel — no separate memset calls.
VAL-DLR-011: _gemv_int4_dual flag is True when kernel loads successfully; engine always uses dual path in decode_step when available.
Both gate and up projections read the same input x[K] in a single pass, saving 1 extra x[K] read from HBM.
SiLU activation is fused into the same kernel for further savings.
Performance: 1.21x speedup for N=11008, 1.08x for N=14336.
Tests cover FFN dims N=4096/11008/14336 (Qwen 3.5 27B FFN) with max_err < 1e-4 in all cases.
Engine wiring test confirms no memset calls.
buffers_clean=True confirms persistent buffer reset works correctly.
